avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs at elevated altitudes. Moist snow slides as the day progresses.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls: The new snow and wind slabs can be released in some cases at intermediate and high altitudes. The avalanches are rather small. Especially shady places that are protected from the wind: Medium-sized avalanches are further to be expected.
At the border to Ribagorça and Pallars the avalanche prone locations are more prevalent.

Sunny slopes and low altitudes: As the day progresses small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized moist snow slides are possible. These can be released by a single winter sport participant or triggered naturally. On steep grassy slopes more small and medium-sized gliding avalanches are possible.

Experience in the assessment of avalanche danger is required.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Monday: The weather will be sunny at times. The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften during the day.

High altitudes: Wind slabs are lying on a hard crust. Towards its base, the snowpack is weak. In particular low altitudes: The snowpack will be generally wet all the way through.

At intermediate altitudes there are 150 to 200 cm of snow, and even more in some localities.

tendency.highlights: Tuesday: The avalanche danger will persist.
